acceptance_criteria.given: The hook processes a bare 'kill -9 1234' command string
acceptance_criteria.then: exit code is 2 (BLOCKED) — kill with signal rule fires
exit code is 2 (BLOCKED) — filesystem rm rule fires
exit code is 2 (BLOCKED) — killall rule fires
exit code is 2 (BLOCKED) — rm in sh -c payload is recursive-analyzed and fires
exit code is 2 (BLOCKED) — kill -signal in bash -c payload is recursive-analyzed and fires

acceptance_criteria.given: The hook processes a multi-line command: bash <<'EOF'\nrm /tmp/foo.txt\nEOF
acceptance_criteria.then: exit code is 2 (BLOCKED) — heredoc consumed by bash is shell context, rm fires
exit code is 2 (BLOCKED) — heredoc piped to bash is shell context
exit code is 2 (BLOCKED) — $() command substitution inside string is executable context

acceptance_criteria.given: The hook processes: echo "literal rm text"; rm /tmp/foo
acceptance_criteria.then: exit code is 2 (BLOCKED) — rm after semicolon is executable, stripper preserves control operator boundaries
exit code is 0 (allowed) — kill token is inside a quoted echo argument, not executable context

acceptance_criteria.given: The hook processes: python3 -c 'import os; # rm is not needed here'
acceptance_criteria.then: exit code is 0 (allowed) — rm appears only after a shell comment marker '#' which is not a control operator; already exits 0 pre-fix (C1 forward-proofing)
acceptance_criteria.pre_fix_behavior: already exits 0 — verified: rule 715 no match, rule 722 no match, rule 743 compound=False (rm|mv present but no workflow path), rule 751 no match (rm preceded by '# ' not a control operator)

check.command: python3 -c 'import os; # rm is not needed here'

acceptance_criteria.then: exit code is 0 (allowed) — heredoc body is piped to tee (non-shell consumer), not executable context

acceptance_criteria.given: The hook processes a command where a non-shell binary's first argument contains 'killall' and a monitored process name as documentation text (e.g. a Skill invocation or documentation command with a prompt body)
acceptance_criteria.then: exit code is 0 (allowed) — token is in string argument to non-shell binary, stripped before matching

check.command: some-doc-tool "document that killall and claude are monitored"

acceptance_criteria.given: The hook processes: git commit -m "fix: avoid SIGKILL by using graceful shutdown"
acceptance_criteria.then: exit code is 0 (allowed) — SIGKILL is in a string argument to git, not executable context; git commit is not blocked by kill rules

check.command: git commit -m "fix: avoid SIGKILL by using graceful shutdown"

acceptance_criteria.given: The hook processes: systemctl restart happy-daemon-dev (no grant sentinel present)
acceptance_criteria.then: exit code is 2 (BLOCKED) — Layer 1.A fires on raw COMMAND, unaffected by context-stripping
exit code is 2 (BLOCKED) — absolute ban fires on raw COMMAND, unaffected by context-stripping
